issue.title: Inquiry Regarding Website Access Issue in AdGuard Environment

labels.name: A: In progress
labels.description: Work on the issue is in progress

labels.name: A: Waiting for data
labels.description: Waiting for more information from the user

issue.body:
### Prerequisites

- [x] I checked the documentation and found no answer;
- [x] I checked to make sure that this issue has not already been filed;
- [x] This is not an ad/bug report.

### Problem description

We operate an advertising network called ACROSS ADN, and we are reaching out because one of our clients reported an issue related to AdGuard while attempting to run advertisements through our network.

After installing the ADN script on the following website, users who are using AdGuard experienced an issue where the website could not be accessed:

https://www.adventurer.co.kr/

At the moment, the ADN script has been removed from the website, and the site is now accessible again.

Below are the environments where the access issue was observed.

[Access Issue]

PC

Website cannot be accessed in browsers with the AdGuard extension installed

iOS

Website cannot be accessed in browsers such as Safari and Chrome

Android

Website cannot be accessed in browsers such as Samsung Internet and Chrome

We would like to ask the following:

Is it possible that a website becomes inaccessible in an AdGuard environment due to an advertising network script?

If this situation can occur, are there any recommended ways to prevent or resolve this issue?

For your reference, we have attached the ADN script file.

We would appreciate your review and any guidance you can provide.

[ADN Script Integration Guide (1).docx](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/26199385/ADN.Script.Integration.Guide.1.docx)


### Proposed solution

If this situation can occur, are there any recommended ways to prevent or resolve this issue?

### Additional information

_No response_

comment.body: I have sent the email to the address you provided. Kindly confirm receipt.

issue.title: www.youtube.com

labels.name: T: Anti Adblock Script
labels.description: The site detects ad blocking

labels.name: NSFW
labels.description: Nudes and porn sites

labels.name: A: Problematic
labels.description: Issues, which cannot be resolved easily

issue.body:
# ATTENTION

> [!NOTE]  
> YouTube changes their detection scripts regularly. That means that sometime you can face the detection message.
> If all the following instructions do not work, please **email filters@adguard.com** or wait until any update is available. 

> [!CAUTION] 
> Please do not use another adblocker in the same time because using another adblocker can delay/lags the YouTube loading (e.g., AdBlock Plus and AdBlock)[^1] OR interfere AdGuard's filtering functionality.

> [!CAUTION] 
> Please do not use an userscript related to the bypassing YouTube detection message like [TheRealJoelmatic/RemoveAdblockThing](https://github.com/TheRealJoelmatic/RemoveAdblockThing).
> Recently, YouTube anti-adblock script added infinite dummy advertisements to try to neutralize the userscript. ( [TheRealJoelmatic/RemoveAdblockThing#264](https://github.com/TheRealJoelmatic/RemoveAdblockThing/issues/264) )

> [!WARNING]
> Please check that there no another web browser extension that has adblocking feature is enabled on your web browser. It can interfere AdGuard's filtering functionality.

> [!IMPORTANT]  
> Saving memory feature of your web browser or sleep feature of your operating system can interfere AdGuard's filtering functionality.
> In this case, please re-enable AdGuard's filtering by toggling the protection toggle.
> Following the instructions can be needed.

### The instructions
1. Force to update the filters by clicking the reload button in AdGuard Browser Extension/AdGuard mobile or update tray menu in your system tray. [^2]
2. Reload the YouTube webpage without cache by pressing <kbd>Ctrl</kbd> + <kbd>Shift</kbd> + <kbd>R</kbd> [^3].
3. Clear cache, cookies and site data of your web browser.
4. Re-open your web browser completely (checking your system monitor[^4] will be better).

[^1]: https://gitlab.com/adblockinc/ext/adblockplus/adblockplusui/-/issues/1576
[^2]: [AdGuard for Windows](https://adguard.com/kb/adguard-for-windows/overview/#tray-menu); [AdGuard for Mac](https://adguard.com/kb/adguard-for-mac/overview/#updates-check); 
[^3]: On macOS, <kbd>Command</kbd> + <kbd>Shift</kbd> + <kbd>R</kbd>
[^4]: Task Manager on Windows, System Monitor on GNOME Shell, KDE System Guard on KDE Plasma or Activity Monitor on macOS. 


Previous issue https://github.com/AdguardTeam/AdguardFilters/issues/198973

comment.body:
`controversychilly.com` is blocked by `||controversychilly.com^` in [`BaseFilter/sections/adservers.txt`](https://github.com/AdguardTeam/AdguardFilters/blob/67b07342e47c553ad7f40d3fb4104b29cff50434/BaseFilter/sections/adservers.txt#L113), which is expected and normal behavior.

Ideally, the notification should not appear at all. It could be prevented cleanly using a prevent-addEventListener, but this has already been implemented in [`TurkishFilter/sections/specific.txt`](https://github.com/AdguardTeam/AdguardFilters/blob/master/TurkishFilter/sections/specific.txt#L191).

Reproduction of the issue is not possible.

labels.name: A: In progress

issue.body:
### Prerequisites

- [x] I checked the documentation and found no answer;
- [x] I checked to make sure that this issue has not already been filed;
- [x] This is not an ad/bug report.

### Problem description

We operate an advertising network called ACROSS ADN, and we are reaching out because one of our clients reported an issue related to AdGuard while attempting to run advertisements through our network.

After installing the ADN script on the following website, users who are using AdGuard experienced an issue where the website could not be accessed:

https://www.adventurer.co.kr/

At the moment, the ADN script has been removed from the website, and the site is now accessible again.

Below are the environments where the access issue was observed.

[Access Issue]

PC

Website cannot be accessed in browsers with the AdGuard extension installed

iOS

Website cannot be accessed in browsers such as Safari and Chrome

Android

Website cannot be accessed in browsers such as Samsung Internet and Chrome

We would like to ask the following:

Is it possible that a website becomes inaccessible in an AdGuard environment due to an advertising network script?

If this situation can occur, are there any recommended ways to prevent or resolve this issue?

For your reference, we have attached the ADN script file.

We would appreciate your review and any guidance you can provide.

[ADN Script Integration Guide (1).docx](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/26199385/ADN.Script.Integration.Guide.1.docx)


### Proposed solution

If this situation can occur, are there any recommended ways to prevent or resolve this issue?

### Additional information

_No response_

comment.body:
Thank you for completing the verification. I have confirmed your identity.

We will begin reviewing whether this is a genuine breakage issue. We will follow up here once we have had a chance to investigate.

Please note that our team is currently short-staffed, so the review may begin early next week. We appreciate your patience.

comment.body:
However, I found that two cases need to be blocked:

### Block `paymoney.click` link replace injection

The site's WordPress `pro-seo-booster` plugin injects an inline script that fires on `DOMContentLoaded`, iterates all links, and wraps their `href` values through `paymoney.click` using `btoa`.

1. The current tab will navigate to `paymoney.click` when clicking the replaced links.
2. A few seconds later, redirect to the `url` param. This is encoded in base64.

### Others

- Hide Telegram channel subscription button on video pages

comment.body:
This issue has been automatically marked as stale because it has been open for 3 days with no activity.
Unfortunately, our resources are limited and we can not always take the time to respond to requests about websites that are not popular enough. The issue will be closed if no further activity occurs.
Please note, that AdGuard Filters is an open-source project that is used by different content blockers and we welcome contributions. If you have some technical knowledge about how websites work, you are welcome to create a pull request following [these instructions](https://github.com/AdguardTeam/AdguardFilters/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md).
